test_suite.description: grades_df has the correct columns, names, and scores
test_suite.hint_not_defined: Define a DataFrame called grades_df. Check the spelling of the variable name.
test_suite.hint_wrong_type: grades_df should be a pandas DataFrame, not a list, dict, or numpy array.
test_suite.hint_wrong_size: grades_df should have columns ['name', 'score', 'passed'] and 5 rows -- one per student.
test_suite.hint_nans: grades_df contains NaN values in a numeric column. Check your score values.

test_suite.hint_not_defined: Define a DataFrame called grades_df. Check the spelling of the variable name.
test_suite.hint_wrong_type: grades_df should be a pandas DataFrame.
test_suite.hint_wrong_size: grades_df should have a 'passed' column with one boolean value per student.
test_suite.hint_tolerance: passed should be True where score >= 70, and False otherwise.

test_suite.description: summary_df has the correct average score per pass/fail group
test_suite.hint_wrong_type: summary_df should be a pandas DataFrame.

test_suite.hint_nans: summary_df contains NaN -- check that every group has at least one student.
